name: Cell-Diffing Render Engine
requirements: IOPort for raw ANSI escape writes

userStory.iWant: the terminal to only receive changed character cells
userStory.soThat: the UI is incredibly responsive on slow SSH connections and does not flicker.

acceptanceCriteria: The engine maintains a 'Current' vs 'Target' surface
A differ computes the minimal set of CUP (Cursor Position) and SGR (Select Graphic Rendition) codes
Sequential changed cells do not repeat CUP codes
Identical styles across multiple cells are batched into a single SGR command
Identical rows are skipped entirely in the output buffer

inScope: ANSI escape code generation and optimization

testPlan.goldenPath: Render a Surface with a single character change. Assert that only 1 CUP move + 1 character code + 1 SGR change are sent to the IOPort.
edgeCases: Resizing the terminal (all cells are now dirty)
Switching between two completely different surfaces
Style changes without character changes (e.g. bolding existing text)
testPlan.fuzzStress: Randomly flip 50% of the bits in a 200x60 surface 60 times a second and measure the total bytes sent via IOPort compared to the old renderer.
failureCases: Inconsistent cursor state leading to characters rendered at wrong coordinates

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(W * H) for full surface comparison
estimatedComplexity.memoryFootprint: 2 * Width * Height * sizeof(Cell) for current/target buffers
